guideposts.label: Welcome to the live coverage of stage 14 of the Tour de France 2026
guideposts.detail: The first contact with the Vosges was focused on the ascent and descent of Ballon d’Alsace, en route to Belfort, where attackers prevailed. Today is a proper rollercoaster with four major climbs and an elevation gain of 3,800 metres packed in155.3 km of racing between Mulhouse and Le Markstein Fellering. The peloton are set to roll at 13:10, to reach km 0 at 13:30. The winners will be crowned at a

guideposts.label: Mulhouse, a classic of the Tour
guideposts.detail: Celebrated for its motor history, Mulhouse became a Tour stage town in 1925. Luxembourg’s Nicolas Frantz took his fourth stage win in that edition, his sixth in total, but Ottavio Bottecchia was head and shoulders above the competition in the overall standings. The Italian claimed his second Tour in a row six days later in Paris, while Frantz had to wait for the 1927 and 1928 editions to prevail.

guideposts.label: Debruyne won't start
guideposts.detail: The Belgian rider from Alpecin-Deceuninck, who is 18th overall, 16’13” behind the Maillot Jaune, and 6th in the young riders' classification after breaking away in the stages to Foix (5th at the finish) and Le Lioran, will not be at the start this morning in Mulhouse due to a combination of “a sore throat and a fever”, according to his team.

guideposts.label: Thinking of Bartali and Clarke
guideposts.detail: A two-time winner of the Tour de France in 1938 and 1948, the legendary Gino Bartali (1914–2000) was born on 18 July in Ponte a Ema, near Florence. In addition to his two victories in the Tour de France, ‘Gino the Pious’ won the Giro d’Italia three times, as well as major races such as Milan – San Remo (four times) and Il Lombardia (three times). His record at the Tour de France includes two King

guideposts.label: Team classification
guideposts.detail: Lidl-Trek still lead the standings but their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is down to 9’18’’. Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe are back in third position overall (+36’58’’).

guideposts.label: Combativity award
guideposts.detail: Tom Pidcock (Pinarello Q36.5) races with a distinctive bib today as he was named the most combative rider yesterday. This honor also served as recognition of the efforts of his teammates Fred Wright, Xandro Meurisse and Quinten Hermans, all three of whom played a significative role in the progress of the breakaway. With this achievement, every team participating in the Tour 2026 has now been award

guideposts.label: Into the final 1,000 kilometres of the Tour 2026
guideposts.detail: Believe it or not, we’ve already covered more than two-thirds of the Tour de France 2026. At km 1.7 of stage 14, there will be just 1,000 kilometres left in this 113th edition of the Grande Boucle.

guideposts.label: First drops of the day
guideposts.detail: It is raining quite heavily on the route of the Tour de France 2026, 4 kilometers from the real start.

guideposts.label: Stage 14 of the Tour de France 2026 is ON!
guideposts.detail: The riders just passed km 0.

guideposts.label: The peloton
Results of the intermediate sprint in Wattwiller (km 12.6)
guideposts.detail: 1. Philipsen, 25 pts 2. Pedersen, 20 pts 3. Kanter, 16 pts 4. Girmay, 14 pts 5. Stewart, 12 pts 6. Planckaert, 10 pts 7. Simmons, 9 pts 8. Teunissen, 8 pts 9. Marsman, 7 pts 10. Vacek, 6 pts 11. Askey, 5 pts 12. Armirail, 4 pts 13. Johannessen, 3 pts 14. Campenaerts, 2 pts 15. Denz, 1 pt

guideposts.label: The first climb of the day
guideposts.detail: Also known as the Ballon de Guebwiller, the Grand Ballon is the highest mountain in the Vosges, despite rising to just 1,424 metres above sea level. In this case, the riders will reach the summit at 1,336 metres above sea level, climbing from Uffholtz. This is the fourth time this mountain pass is tackled via a significant ascent in the Tour de France. However, it was also climbed in 2014 and 2019

guideposts.label: Healy attacks early on the climb
guideposts.detail: The Irish attacker is followed by Tiberi, Foss, Hirschi, T. Johannessen...

guideposts.label: A huge group on the move
guideposts.detail: Antonio Tiberi (Bahrain Victorious) and Tobias Johannessen (Uno-X Mobility) set the pace. We can see Marc Hirschi, Thymen Arensman, Richard Carapaz, Valentin Paret-Peintre, Quinn Simmons, Jan Tratnik, Bruno Armirail...

guideposts.label: 1 minute
guideposts.detail: UAE Emirates-XRG set the pace in the peloton behind a group of around 40 attackers.

guideposts.label: Attacks keep flying
guideposts.detail: With 12 kilometres to the summit, nine riders attack from the front group: Carapaz, Paret-Peintre, Tiberi, Arensman, Hirschi... Meanwhile, others close the gap form the peloton, including Van Eetvelt and Storer.

guideposts.label: Healy and Castrillo set off
guideposts.detail: They attack from the breakaway on a downhill section halfway up the climb. Meanwhile, Van Eetvelt bridges the gap to the chase group. His teammate Craps is also here.

guideposts.label: An attack by Ben Healy, followed by Pablo Castrillo
30 kilometres into the stage
guideposts.detail: Healy and Castrillo are 10 seconds ahead of a group with around 30 riders. Higuita, Martin-Guyonnet and Pacher trail by 50 seconds. UAE Emirates XRG control the gap to the bunch: 1'25''.

guideposts.label: The final 5 kilometres on Grand Ballon
guideposts.detail: Ben Healy and Pablo Castrillo are reeled in by the chase group paced by Victor Campenaerts. The bunch trails by 1'30''. The'yve caught Martin-Guyonnet. Higuita and Pacher are just ahead.

guideposts.label: The two riders at the front have been caught by the chasing group
34.9 kilometres in the first hour
guideposts.detail: That includes the first 20 kilometres up Grand Ballon.

guideposts.label: VPP conquers the first KOM of the day
guideposts.detail: The French Climber got the better of Carapaz at the summit.

guideposts.label: The gap at the summit: 1'40''
guideposts.detail: Nils Politt set the pace all the way to the summit.

guideposts.label: Results at Grand Ballon (cat. 1, km 36.6)
guideposts.detail: 1. V. Paret-Peintre, 10 pts 2. Carapaz, 8 pts 3. Healy, 6 pts 4. T. Johannessen, 4 pts 5. A. Johannessen, 2 pts 6. Vauquelin, 1 pt

guideposts.label: The leaders cross the finish line for the first time today
guideposts.detail: They still have 111.4 km to cover. +30'' Arensman, Castrillo, Rubio +50'' Chase group +1'40'' Peloton

guideposts.label: The five leaders have crossed the finish line for the first time
The peloton crosses the finish line
The situation on the downhill
guideposts.detail: The five leaders will soon enter the final 100 kilometres of the stage. A handful of chasers try to close the gap. The Pidcock group trail by 1'15''. The gap to the bunch is up to 1'55''. Anthon Charmig (Uno-X Mobility) just crossed the finish line 9'05'' behind the leaders. The Danish puncheur won the combativity award on day 11 and crashed the next day in the finale.

guideposts.label: Summit (cat 1) - 1342m
Mechanical for Arensman
guideposts.detail: Netcompany Ineos' Dutch attacker calls for assistance. He seems to have a rear wheel puncture. But he keeps pushing on the ascent as he waits for his team's vehicle.

guideposts.label: Arensman stops to take a new bike
guideposts.detail: At the same moment, Castrillo stops as he has given everything he had in the chase. Rubio is 25 seconds behind the leaders, and Arensman is now trying to get back to the Colombian climber. +1'50'' Pidcock-Jorgenson group +2'20'' Peloton

guideposts.label: Rubio joins the 5 leaders
guideposts.detail: They are inside the final 5 kilometres of ascent. Arensman is 25 seconds behind. Armirail pacesthe chase group with a gap of 1'55''. Campenaerts has been dropped and caught by the bunch (+2'30'').

guideposts.label: Politt still sets the pace in the bunch
guideposts.detail: The gap increases to 2'50''. They're catching Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) and Damien Howson (Pinarello-Q36.5) while the leaders enter the final kilometre of ascent.

guideposts.label: Summit (cat 2) - 955m
Paret-Peintre conquers another summit
guideposts.detail: The French climber is up to 33 KOM points after going first at Col du Page. Pogacar leads the mountains classification with 42 points.

guideposts.label: Results at Col du Page (cat. 2, km 71.3)
guideposts.detail: 1. V. Paret-Peintre, 5 pts 2. Carapaz, 3 pts 3. T. Johannessen, 2 pts 4. Rubio, 1 pt Arensman crosses the summit with a gap of 35''. +1'50'' Pidcock group, paced by Armirail

guideposts.label: The average speed after two hours: 39.5 km/h
guideposts.detail: Richard Carapaz, Ben Healy (EF Education-EasyPost), Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step), Tobias Johannessen and Anders Johannessen (Uno-X Mobility) have covered 44.6 km in the second hour. The gap to Thymen Arensman (Netcompany Ineos) has increased to 50 seconds.

guideposts.label: The packs get back together on the valley
guideposts.detail: Lidl-Trek madesure they didn't let Remco Evenepoel (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe) get away.

guideposts.label: Onto the Ballon d'Alsace
guideposts.detail: Ben Healy and Richard Carapaz started the ascent a few metres behind after they missed a turn. The iconic summit was the first major pass on the route of the Tour, back in 1905. Today marks the 30th ascent on Ballon d'Alsace. The 29th was yesterday.

guideposts.label: The peloton start the ascent with a gap of 3 minutes
guideposts.detail: They get back to Georg Steinhauser (EF Education-EasyPost). The Pidcock group trail by 1'50''.

guideposts.label: Arensman waits for the bunch
guideposts.detail: The Dutch attacker was aiming to repeat his success from stage 14 of the Tour de France 2025.

guideposts.label: Paul Seixas (DECATHLON CMA CGM)
Inside the last 5 kilometres of ascent
guideposts.detail: The Pidcock group get back to Arensman. Armirail still sets the pace 1'45'' behind the leaders. Hirschi is back with them.

guideposts.label: 1 km between the leaders and the peloton
guideposts.detail: Valentin Paret-Peintre sets the pace into the last 2 kilometres of ascent. Flix Grossschartner drives the bunch 3 km kilometres from the summit. They are less than 30 seconds behind the Pidcock group.

guideposts.label: V. Paret-Peintre takes the lead of the KOM standings
guideposts.detail: The French climber is up to 43 points, including 25 claimed across today's first three ascents. Pogacar has 42 points, and Carapaz has 38 points.

guideposts.label: Summit (cat 1) - 1173m
The gaps at the summit
guideposts.detail: At 1'50'', Matteo Jorgenson (Visma-Lease a Bike) is now setting the pace himself in the chase group. The peloton are only 10 seconds behind them, with Felix Grossschartner still at the helm. He's followed by Brandon McNulty while Tim Wellens calls for the team car.

guideposts.label: Results at Ballon d'Alsace (cat 1, km 94.4)
guideposts.detail: 1. V. Paret-Peintre, 10 pts 2. Carapaz, 8 pts 3. T. Johannessen, 6 pts 4. Rubio, 4 pts 5. Healy, 2 pts 6. A. Johannessen, 1 pt

guideposts.label: The chasers are caught by the bunch after the summit
guideposts.detail: The five leaders are 2 minutes ahead.

guideposts.label: This crowd
Into the final 50 kilometres
guideposts.detail: The six leaders maintain a gap of 2'05'' to the peloton. THey will soon face an uncategorised ascent: Col du Hundsruck, which begins 38.5 kilometres from the finish and will involve an 8.4-kilometre climb with an average gradient of 4 per cent, with a 500-metre break once we reach the summit of the Col du Schirm, 34.9 km from the finish

guideposts.label: The Pidcock group has been caught by the peloton
Martinez and Bernal are caught behind
guideposts.detail: Bahrain Victorious drive the chase while Tim Wellens (UAE Emirates XRG) set the pace at the front of a 25-man bunch.

guideposts.label: There’s been a break in the peloton
40 kilometres to go for the leaders
guideposts.detail: +1'45'' GC group +2'05'' Martinez-Bernal-Jegat group

guideposts.label: The two packs get back together
guideposts.detail: They are 1'30'' behind the leaders as the road rises to Col d'Hundsruck.

guideposts.label: The peloton regrouped
Le Markstein, a special memory for Pogacar
guideposts.detail: “I’m really looking forward to riding at Le Markstein and to enjoy the crowd”, Tadej Pogacar said as he prepared to return to a venue he had already conquered. It was back in 2023, on stage 20, the first to finish there. At the beginning of the third week, the Slovenian, a 2-time winner of the Tour at the time, had suffered the rule of Jonas Vingegaard in the Combloux ITT (stage 16) and the follow

guideposts.label: The average speed after 3 hours: 40.3 km/h
guideposts.detail: The leaders covered 41.9 km in the last hour. The last gap: 1'35''.

guideposts.label: Healy is done
guideposts.detail: Five riders remain at the front with 32.5 km to go: Richard Carapaz (EF Eductaion-EasyPost), Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step), Tobias and Anders Johannessen (Uno-X Mobility), and Einer Rubio (Movistar).

guideposts.label: Le Markstein, also an iconic venue of the Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift
guideposts.detail: Tadej Pogacar already had nice memories from today’s finish even before he took victory for himself: “The first time I heard about Markstein was when Urska [Zigart] climbed in the top 10 at the Tour de France”, the Slovenian star said this morning, reminiscing the finale of the Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift 2022 and his partner’s performance. To cap off the return of a women’s Tour de France, t

guideposts.label: The final 25 kilometres
guideposts.detail: The peloton are 1'25'' behind the five leaders. The final climb begins 17.1 kilometres from the finish.

guideposts.label: The debut of the Col du Haag
guideposts.detail: It is one of the many roads that climb the Grand Ballon; probably the newest, as it was only tarmacked in the summer of 2023. This climb begins at Saint-Amarin (km 138.2) and follows a road with an uneven surface and challenging gradients, levelling off as it passes through the village of Geishouse. The most spectacular moment comes just afterwards, 4.5 kilometres from the summit, when the route e

guideposts.label: Prodhomme pulls the bunch
guideposts.detail: On his wheel: Pogacar, Vingegaard, Lipowitz, Evenepoel, Seixas, Skjelmose, Del Toro, Martinez, Ayuso, Kuss, Voisard.

guideposts.label: Carapaz attacks
guideposts.detail: The Ecuadorian goes solo with just under 9 kilometres remaining on the ascent. Paret-Peintre and Rubio are 30 seconds behind. The gap to the peloton is down to 55 seconds.

guideposts.label: Carapaz is the most combative rider of the day
guideposts.detail: The Ecuadorian climber is 12 km from teh finish. +10'' Johannessen +55'' GC group. Paret-Peintre and Rubio have been caught and distanced.

guideposts.label: Lipowitz attacks 5.5 km from the summit
guideposts.detail: Seixas immediately reacts. Kuss drives the chasewith Vingegaard on his wheel. They're followed by Pogacar, Ayuso, Martinez, and Del Toro.

guideposts.label: Vingegaard accelerates
guideposts.detail: Pogacar is on his wheel. Skjelmose and Martinez have lost a few metres.

guideposts.label: Lipowitz launches an attack
Evenepoel can't keep up with the pace set by Vingegaard
guideposts.detail: Del Toro also struggles.

guideposts.label: Johannessen catches Carapaz 3 km from the summit
guideposts.detail: Vingegaard brinsgs the dap down to 23''. Evenepoel is right behind.

guideposts.label: Johannessen attacks Carapaz
guideposts.detail: The Ecuadorian climber resists. The GC group are less than 10 seconds behind.

guideposts.label: Carapaz and Johannessen are caught by Vingegaard, Pogacar, Seixas, Ayuso, Lipowitz, and Del Toro
guideposts.detail: Evenepoel is 25 seconds behind. 2.5 km to to go to the summit.

guideposts.label: Tadej Pogacar attacks
Summit (cat 1) - 1230m
Pogacar attacks on the steepest section
guideposts.detail: The gradients reach 16% with just under 2 km left on the climb. Vingegaard drives the chase, with Lipowitz, Ayruso, Seixas and Carapaz on his wheel.

guideposts.label: Last kilometre of ascent
guideposts.detail: Vingegaard has accelerated. And Seixas has set off in pursuit of the Dane.

guideposts.label: Vingegaard has launched an attack
Paul Seixas catches up with Jonas Vingegaard
Evenepoel is back with Ayuso and Lipowitz
guideposts.detail: They are 50 seconds behind Pogacar.

guideposts.label: Isaac Del Toro has caught up with the Vingegaard-Seixas duo
Pogacar is riding to a 25th Tour win
guideposts.detail: He would join André Leducq as the 4th best scorer in Tour history.

guideposts.label: Pogacar conquers the Col du Haag
Tadej Pogacar wins stage 14 of the Tour de France 2026
guideposts.detail: His fourth win this year.

guideposts.label: Finish - km 155.2
Top 5
guideposts.detail: 1. Tadej Pogacar (UAE Emirates XRG) 2. Isaac del Toro (UAE Emirates XRG), +38'' 3. Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM), +38'' 4. Jonas Vingegaard (Visma-Lease a Bike), +44'' 5. Remco Evenepoel (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe), +48''
